title: Smart-seq3 Protocol
author: Michael Hagemann-Jensen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bcq4ivyw

steps.title: This protocol should be carried out in a clean environment
steps.instruction: This protocol should be carried out in a clean environment. Use ethanol, RNAseZAP, DNA-OFF, or similar to prepare work bench before start. Work quickly and preferably on On ice . Prepare master-mixes right before use. Use multichannel pipettes, liquid dispensers etc. to dispense the master-mixes. Avoid pipetting up and down, to minimize the loss of material. • Take a look at the Guidelines section for more info about Oligos etc. used in this protocol.

steps.title: Prepare lysis buffer mix %s %s...
steps.instruction: Prepare lysis buffer mix %s %s The lysis master-mix contains PEG! Ensure that PEG is fully mixed into solution, by either pipetting up and down until the liquid is clear, or start with vortexing the required master-mix volume of water and PEG together before adding the remaining reagents. Add 3 µL lysis buffer to each well of a 96/384 well plate. Quick centrifugation to collect the lysis buffer before storage until use.

steps.title: Sort single cells into 3 µL...
steps.instruction: Sort single cells into 3 µL lysis in either 96 or 384 wells. Seal with appropriate seals (-80 C to >100 C) and centrifuge the finished sorted plate immediately after, and transfer it to a -80 °C freezer or dry-ice.

steps.title: Remove the plate of sorted cells...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate of sorted cells from the -80 freezer and incubate in a thermocycler with heated lid at 72 °C for 00:10:00 , followed by a 4 °C hold (keeping the storage seal sheet on the plate, unless damaged or loose).

steps.title: While the plate is incubating as...
steps.instruction: While the plate is incubating as per step 4 , prepare the following Reverse transcription master-mix . %s %s Add 1 µL RT mix to each well of a 96/384 well plate. Replace the storage seal with a PCR seal. Ensure that the plate is properly sealed, to avoid evaporation. Do a quick centrifugation to collect reaction at the bottom, before incubating the plate in a thermocycler at; %s %s

steps.title: Start preparing the PCR mix, when...
steps.instruction: Start preparing the PCR mix, when the incubation of the reverse transcription reaction is near completion, by combining the following components. ! Note that the KAPA DNA polymerase has a 3-5' exonuclease activity that is not HotStart. Therefore add polymerase just before using the master-mix. %s %s Add 6 µL PCR mix to each well of a 96/384 well plate. Quick centrifugation to collect reaction at the bottom, before running the following PCR program in a thermocycler. %s %s

steps.title: Before purification prepare 22% PEG Clean-up...
steps.instruction: Before purification prepare 22% PEG Clean-up Beads used for cleaning up the preamplified cDNA . These beads perform similar to Ampure XP beads. Beads are prepared as per mcSCRB-seq protocol Johannes Bagnoli, Christoph Ziegenhain, Aleksandar Janjic, Lucas Esteban Wange, Beate Vieth, Swati Parekh, Johanna Geuder, Ines Hellmann, Wolfgang Enard. mcSCRB-seq protocol. Nature Communications. http://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.p9kdr4w %s %s AB1 ReagentAmount2PEG 800011 g 3NaCl, 5M 10 mL4Tris-HCL, 1M, pH 8.0 500 μL5EDTA, 0.5M 100 μL 6IGEPAL, 10% solution 50 μL7Sodium Azide, 10% solution 250 μL8UltraPure Waterup to 49 mL9Total 49 mL Add all ingredients into a 50 mL falcon tube, but do not add the total amount of water until after PEG is completely solubilized. Incubate at 40 °C and vortex regularly until PEG is completely dissolved. Resuspend bead stock carefully (Sera-Mag Speed Beads). Pipette 1000 µL of bead suspension into a 1.5 mL tube. Place on magnet stand Remove supernatant. Add 1000 µL 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA (TE), and resuspend beads. Place on magnet stand. Remove supernatant. Repeat wash one more time. Add 900 µL 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA (TE), and resuspend beads. Add to PEG solution above and mix well.

steps.title: To purify cDNA add 0
steps.instruction: To purify cDNA add 0.6:1 ratio of 22% PEG beads to sample, and mix by gently pipetting up and down.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:08:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:08:00 .

steps.title: Place on magnet and allow beads to settle
steps.instruction: Place on magnet and allow beads to settle. Roughly 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Discard supernatant, and wash once with...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant, and wash once with 20 µL / 100 µL of freshly prepared 80% Ethanol for 384 / 96 well plates respectively.

steps.title: Remove Ethanol and let the beads...
steps.instruction: Remove Ethanol and let the beads air dry for 00:02:00 - 00:05:00

steps.title: Elute cDNA in 12 µL UltraPure...
steps.instruction: Elute cDNA in 12 µL UltraPure Water, resuspend beads and incubate for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Prepare 1X TE buffer by either...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1X TE buffer by either diluting the 20X TE buffer from the QuantiFluor® dsDNA kit or by preparing a solution of 10mM Tris-HCl, 0.1mM EDTA, pH

steps.title: Dilute the QuantiFluor® dsDNA Dye 1:400...
steps.instruction: Dilute the QuantiFluor® dsDNA Dye 1:400 in 1X TE buffer and mix.

steps.title: Prepare dsDNA standards for plate read-out, according to manufacturers protocol
steps.instruction: Prepare dsDNA standards for plate read-out, according to manufacturers protocol.

steps.title: Dispense 49 µL / 99 µL...
steps.instruction: Dispense 49 µL / 99 µL per well of the ready Quantiflour dye mix into black, flat-bottom 384/96 well plates, respectively.

steps.title: Add Standards to a separate plate
steps.instruction: Add Standards to a separate plate.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of cDNA to each well
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of cDNA to each well. Incubate asssay for 00:05:00 at Room temperature

steps.title: Use a plate reader, to measure...
steps.instruction: Use a plate reader, to measure fluorescence (504nM Excitation/ 531nM Emission)

steps.title: Calculate cDNA concentration
steps.instruction: Calculate cDNA concentration.

steps.title: Calculate water needed to dilute 1...
steps.instruction: Calculate water needed to dilute 1 µL cDNA to 100 pg/uL .
1. Prepare normalization plate by adding the calculated water volumes to each well. 2. Add 1 µL of preamplified cDNA to each well.

steps.title: Check the cDNA preamplification library content...
steps.instruction: Check the cDNA preamplification library content and quality on a Agilent Bioanalzyer, using High Sensitivity DNA Analysis chips. Example of one HEK cell.

steps.title: Prepare 4x Tagmentation buffer as following
steps.instruction: Prepare 4x Tagmentation buffer as following. Aliquots of 4xTD buffer can be stored for later use. The TD buffer (2x) from Nextera Kits can also be used, however with the current small amount of ATM used, the Illumina TD buffer will at some point run out. Dimethylformamide (DMF) should be handled in a fume hood and according to local safety regulations. %s %s

steps.title: Prepare Tagmentation mix
steps.instruction: Prepare Tagmentation mix. • Please note that the ATM amount is a suggested starting point for 100pg/uL input, and some optimization might be necessary to reach a desired UMI-read to Internal-read ratio, based on input and celltype.. %s %s

steps.title: Dispense 1 µL of Tagmentation mix...
steps.instruction: Dispense 1 µL of Tagmentation mix to a new 96 or 384 well plate.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of normalized 100pg/uL cDNA (
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of normalized 100pg/uL cDNA (

steps.title: to the plate containing tagmentation mix
steps.instruction: to the plate containing tagmentation mix.

steps.title: Apply a quick spin-down of the...
steps.instruction: Apply a quick spin-down of the plate before incubation in a thermocycler at 55 °C for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: To strip off the Tn5 from the DNA, add 0
steps.instruction: To strip off the Tn5 from the DNA, add 0.5 µL of 0.2% SDS to each well. Centrifuge quickly and incubate for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Concerning Nextera Index primers
steps.instruction: Concerning Nextera Index primers: We highly suggest to design or order custom Nextera Index primers. This ensures higher flexibility while also being much cheaper in the long run! The following protocol is designed as such. If using Nextera index primers purchased from Illumina, dilute all primers 5x with UltraPure water, and proceed to use similar volume as follows.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 µL Nextera Index primers t o each well as follows %s %s

steps.title: Prepare Tagmentation PCR mix
steps.instruction: Prepare Tagmentation PCR mix . %s %s

steps.title: Add 3 µL of Tagmentation PCR...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL of Tagmentation PCR mix to each well, centrifuge quickly and incubate in a thermocycler using the following PCR program. %s %s

steps.title: sample wells in a 1
steps.instruction: sample wells in a 1.5mL or 5mL eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.6:1 22% PEG beads to final volume of the pooled tagmentation cDNA . Mix gently by pipetting and incubate for 00:08:00 at Room temperature

steps.title: Place on magnet and allow beads to settle
steps.instruction: Place on magnet and allow beads to settle. Roughly 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Discard supernatant, and wash twice with...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant, and wash twice with >= 1000 µL freshly prepared 80% Ethanol.

steps.title: Remove Ethanol and let the beads...
steps.instruction: Remove Ethanol and let the beads air dry for at least 00:05:00

steps.title: Elute cDNA in 40 µL UltraPure...
steps.instruction: Elute cDNA in 40 µL UltraPure Water, resuspend beads and incubate for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Load 20 µL of the eluted...
steps.instruction: Load 20 µL of the eluted tagmented library from step 13 into a 2% Agarose E-Gel EX together with 50bp DNA ladder.

steps.title: Run gel for 00:12:00
steps.instruction: Run gel for 00:12:00

steps.title: After finished run, open the gel...
steps.instruction: After finished run, open the gel casing and cut the gel between 550bp - 2kb using a clean scalpel or blade.

steps.title: Purify the excised gel slice using...
steps.instruction: Purify the excised gel slice using Qiagen QIAquick Gel extraction kit according to manufacturers protocol.

steps.title: Run the final library on a...
steps.instruction: Run the final library on a Agilent Bioanalyzer (High Sensitivity DNA), to inspect the quality and median base-pair length of your library. Suggestive example of a finshed HEK library. A bit on the large size. Can still be sequenced. Use Qubit fluorometer or similar to quantify the library. Calcutate the final library concentration, using above metrics.

steps.title: The sequencing ready library should be...
steps.instruction: The sequencing ready library should be sequenced on any Illumina compatible sequencer, either Single-end or Paired-end, depending on the question and need. For final library whether gel cut/size selected or not, the expected median base-pair should be around or above 1kb. In our experience NovaSeq/HiSeq sequncers are more tolerant towards wider or longer size fragment distributions, than the NextSeq. Because of this consider increasing the loading concentration a bit to ensure proper cluster density. However "your milage may vary". Empircal investigation or a pilot run is always adviced, if possible.

steps.title: After sequencing has completed successfully, binary...
steps.instruction: After sequencing has completed successfully, binary base-call files (BCL) need to be converted to fastq. For this, bcl2fastq should be used in the latest version (bcl2fastq v2.20). bcl2fastq 2.20 Illumina At this stage, demultiplexing into per-cell fastq files is not necessary - a sample sheet is thus not needed. Be sure to adjust the base mask to represent your sequencing layout and the length of your barcode reads. Remove the option --no-lane-splitting if the same cell barcodes have been reused for different libraries on different lanes of the flow cell. You may restrict the number of cores used with the following options: --loading-threads --processing-threads --writing-threads bcl2fastq: 2x150bp dual-index bcl2fastq --use-bases-mask Y150N,I8,I8,Y150N --no-lane-splitting --create-fastq-for-index-reads -R /mnt/storage1/NextSeqNAS/191011_NB502120_0154_AHVG7JBGXB -R denotes the runfolder and you may redirect the fastq output to a different folder with the -o option.

steps.title: name
steps.instruction: name: /smartseq3/fastq/Undetermined_S0_R1_001.fastq.gz base_definition: - cDNA(23-150) - UMI(12-

steps.title: find_pattern
steps.instruction: find_pattern: ATTGCGCAATG file

steps.title: name
steps.instruction: name: /smartseq3/fastq/Undetermined_S0_R2_001.fastq.gz base_definition: - cDNA(1-

steps.title: name
steps.instruction: name: /smartseq3/fastq/Undetermined_S0_I1_001.fastq.gz base_definition: - BC(1-

steps.title: name
steps.instruction: name: /smartseq3/fastq/Undetermined_S0_I2_001.fastq.gz base_definition: - BC(1-8)reference: STAR_index: /resources/genomes/Mouse/STAR5idx_noGTF/ GTF_file: /resources/genomes/Mouse/Mus_musculus.GRCm38.91.gtf additional_STAR_params: '--clip3pAdapterSeq CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT' additional_files: - /resources/genomes/spikes/ERCC92.faout_dir: /smartseq3/zUMIs/num_threads: 20mem_limit: 50filter_cutoffs: BC_filter: num_bases: 3 phred: 20 UMI_filter: num_bases: 2 phred: 20barcodes: barcode_num: ~ barcode_file: /smartseq3/expected_barcodes.txt automatic: no BarcodeBinning: 1 nReadsperCell: 100 demultiplex: nocounting_opts: introns: yes downsampling: '0' strand: 0 Ham_Dist: 1 write_ham: no velocyto: no primaryHit: yes twoPass: nomake_stats: yeswhich_Stage: Filteringsamtools_exec: samtoolspigz_exec: pigzSTAR_exec: STARRscript_exec: Rscript Be sure to use full paths to all files and folders. For further descriptions of the individual options visit the zUMIs GitHub repository wiki Now, simply start zUMIs with the following command: Invoke zUMIs zUMIs-master.sh -y Smartseq3.yaml
